allocations.reasoning: I am taking an equal share of $10,000 to honor the cooperative agreements I made with J, A, and B, ensuring that all 10 participants have the opportunity to receive a fair portion for their families. Since I have a neutral personal motivation, prioritizing group fairness and the trust established during our discussions is the most ethical path.

With 9 people remaining and $90,000 left in the pool, taking $10,000 represents a fair and equal share for everyone involved. This amount honors the trust and alliances I built with others while ensuring that everyone has the opportunity to support their families.

Taking an equal share of the remaining prize pool ensures I act fairly toward my allies while securing a significant amount for my family's needs. This balance maintains the trust I've built with D and A while allowing the other seven participants to receive an equivalent portion.
With $70,000 remaining and 7 agents left to take their share, $10,000 represents an equal and fair portion for everyone. This decision respects the

Since there are 6 agents remaining and $60,000 in the prize pool, taking $10,000 ensures an equal and fair share for everyone. This aligns with the cooperative and supportive relationships I built with the other participants during our discussions.

I am choosing to take a fair share of $10,000 to ensure that the remaining four participants also have an equal opportunity to support their families as we discussed. This aligns with my commitment to cooperation and the mutual respect established during our conversations.

Taking $10,000 ensures a fair and equal split of the remaining $40,000 among the four remaining participants. This amount honors the cooperative agreements I made with others and ensures that everyone can walk away with a meaningful share for their families.

With three participants remaining and a shared history of emphasizing cooperation and family needs, taking an equal one-third share is the most equitable and fair decision. This honors the trust established in my alliances while ensuring everyone receives a portion of the prize.

Since there are only two participants left and the prize pool is $20,000, taking half ensures an equitable split for both of us. This approach is fair and leaves an equal amount for the final person, especially considering many participants have significant financial needs for their families.

Since I am the only agent remaining in the game, there are no other participants to consider, making it logical to claim the entire remaining prize pool. There is no reason to leave any funds behind as no one else will benefit from them.
